description_long: This object is a partially melted, off-white candle with a visible black wick protruding from the top. The candle has an irregular, organic shape with a bulbous base and a narrower, slightly uneven upper section, suggesting it has been used and has melted unevenly. The surface displays subtle indentations and minor deformations, characteristic of wax that has softened and re-solidified. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving it a simple, utilitarian appearance. The candle does not appear to be housed in any holder or container, and the base is slightly wider to provide stability when placed upright. The overall look is that of a standard wax candle suitable for general decorative or emergency use in urban environments.
description: A partially melted, off-white candle with a visible black wick and a bulbous base, featuring an irregular, organic wax surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the candle's bulbous base and the slightly uneven, melted top with the wick clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the irregular melting and subtle indentations along the candle's body.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the candle's overall symmetry and the continuation of the bulbous base, with the wick still visible.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the organic, uneven surface and the tapering from the base to the top.
